items.title: 'Straight to landfill': Compostable packaging but nowhere to compost it

children.content: The ACT mandates reusable, recyclable and compostable packaging at all public events, but nearly all of it ends up in landfill because there is no facility to compost it.

gallery.altText: A meal served on a compostable paper plate with wooden cutlery.

thumbnail.altText: A meal served on a compostable paper plate with wooden cutlery.

items.title: A man fled a 'bounty' in Canberra. Months later, he was dead in Brisbane

children.content: Neil Tracey's death was ruled an overdose in what investigators would call an open and shut case. His family are questioning if police knew about the bounty that led him to flee his hometown.

image.altText: Graphic of a man's smiling face with newspapers articles surrounding it.

items.title: Court hears of 'tragic mistake' that caused fatal Canberra crash

children.content: The daughters of a motorcyclist killed in Canberra's north tried to reach him for their nightly phone call as he lay dying on the road, an ACT court has heard.

image.altText: A man with a beard smiles, wearing a hat that says Harley Davidson, sitting inside an eatery.

items.title: Heated Rivalry, Off Campus driving ice hockey boom in Canberra

children.content: Women now outnumber men as attendees to Canberra Brave ice hockey games — thanks in part to a surge in popularity for the sport inspired by the recent shows Heated Rivalry and Off Campus.

gallery.altText: Two men in tuxedos with their foreheads touching.

items.title: Budget blowout in Queanbeyan with 100yo pipe found in unexpected spot

children.content: A century-old stormwater drain nearly 2 metres thick is uncovered beneath Queanbeyan's town centre, forcing the council to extend major roadworks and source extra funding.

image.altText: An old pip dug up in the ground.

items.title: Parton says Canberrans are 'more vulnerable' after budget's release

children.content: The Canberra Liberals and the ACT Greens issue their responses to the ACT budget, criticising the government's fiscal plan, principally around cost of living, transport and overall management of the territory's finances.

image.altText: Mark Parton and Leanne Castley

items.title: Canberra's only water park will go up for sale. Here's why that's good news

children.content: The ACT government says the owners are not Canberra-based and had thought they were investing in something very different.

gallery.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

thumbnail.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

items.title: Trump ring-side as first White House UFC fight ends in dramatic KO

children.content: As the fighters prepare for their South Lawn debuts, the Weather Channel is predicting heavy rain and potential thunderstorms. Follow live.

image.altText: Diego Lopes stands on top of the octagon at the White House.

items.title: 'Absolutely terrified': Scolyer's doctor reveals fears of experimental therapy

children.content: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.

thumbnail.altText: Man in a blue-collared button-up shirt sits in a dining room chair, a neutral facial expression, a lamp in background

items.title: Police treating deaths of man and girl at Sydney bay as murder-suicide

children.content: Investigators are trying to determine what led to a suspected murder-suicide on Saturday, after the bodies of a father and young child were recovered from a bay off Concord.

image.altText: A boat on a dock, with police tape in front and two officers examining the scene.

items.title: China's ability to hit Australia with missiles 'real and growing'

children.content: New analysis warns that China's military is developing a "real and growing" ability to hit the Australian mainland with missiles, and can already threaten Australia's trade routes, subsea cables and critical infrastructure.

image.altText: Military vehicles carrying DF-26 ballistic missiles during a military parade in China.

items.title: Ireland is a data centre 'cautionary tale'. This is the impact on locals

children.content: As Australia and other countries embark on a new wave of AI-driven data centre development, locals in Ireland share how the runaway growth of the facilities can affect everyday life.

articles.title: Singapore-based AI company to become Tasmania's largest power user

children.content: Artificial intelligence company Firmus Technologies will become Tasmania's biggest power user if its ambitions to establish three "AI factories" in the state come to fruition.

image.altText: A high tech room.

items.title: Is SpaceX worth $3 trillion? Of course not, but that's not the point

children.content: To get a sense of the difference between the Tesla and SpaceX IPOs, consider that 1.7 billion seconds ago was 1972, when Elton John released Rocket Man, while 1.7 trillion seconds ago was 52,000BC, when we shared the planet with Neanderthals.

image.altText: Elon Musk wearing a suit behind a dark backdrop.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Violence victim unable to break lease without paying

children.content: Annie feared for her safety from a new next-door neighbour in northern Tasmania but was told she couldn't break her lease without continuing to pay rent. She was then sexually assaulted.

image.altText: A woman sits on a bench on the foreshore

items.title: Multi-billion-dollar economic policy undermined by 'black cladding'

children.content: Almost $15 billion has been spent over a decade via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of "black cladding".

image.altText: An Aboriginal flag flying outside of parliament house

items.title: Buyer's agency clients urged to proceed days before collapse

children.content: Emails and messages seen by ABC News show Dashdot's staff were still encouraging prospective customers to engage with the buyers agency shortly before it entered voluntary liquidation.

image.altText: Man with a serious expressions sits at a table with a laptop in front of him.

items.title: Prolonged pause on interest rates possible as RBA board meets for June

children.content: Most experts are tipping interest rates will remain on hold but the question is when will they come down.

image.altText: Aerial view of rows of new houses in outer suburbia.

items.title: Business groups urge rejection of CGT changes

children.content: Business groups are urging the government to scrap its capital gains tax changes as the first tranche of measures is scrutinised at a parliamentary inquiry.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers close up looking serious with Australian flag in background.

items.title: Palestinians fear 'archaeology has become a tool' in Israel's plans for West Bank

children.content: Palestinians believe there is more to Israel's recently announced plans to develop heritage sites along the West Bank, arguing archaeology has "become a tool, a mechanism, to justify settlement and justify the displacement of Palestinians" across the area.

gallery.altText: A Palestinian flag hangs above Sebastia.

thumbnail.altText: A Palestinian flag hangs above Sebastia.

items.title: Picturesque islands in WA's north set to become transport hubs after $20m sale

children.content: A Kimberley iron ore mine with idyllic views across a tropical sea will close after 70 years of operation as a new phase begins for offshore oil and gas.

image.altText: aerial of a bright blue ocean and a green red island

items.title: 'Worrying' surge in people injecting peptides in WA

children.content: Needle exchanges in Western Australia are reporting dramatic increases in people accessing their services for peptide use.

image.altText: A collection of small clear vials containing different coloured liquid.

items.title: Travellers owed $4.3 million as dream holidays shattered

children.content: Australian travellers who collectively lost millions after a travel agency collapsed are calling for an overhaul of consumer protections in the sector.

items.title: Twelve killed as skydiving plane goes down shortly after take-off

children.content: The private plane had just taken off and made a turn before it crashed and caught fire, authorities said.

image.altText: A drone view of a crashed plane in a field next to a highway

items.title: Woman dies in rope jump fall in Brazil

children.content: In videos of the accident circulating online, two men hoist the 21-year-old above their heads and launch her off a bridge in São Paulo.

image.altText: A police car parked in front of a glass building

items.title: Chemicals in Antarctic huts pose 'potential explosive' risk

children.content: Chemicals found inside two of Antarctica's most important historical sites, Earnest Shackleton and Robert Falcon Scott's huts, could pose "potential explosive hazards", a report has warned.

gallery.altText: Shackleton's Nimrod Hut is nestled in the landscape at at Cape Royds, Mount Erebus is shrouded in cloud in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Shackleton's Nimrod Hut is nestled in the landscape at at Cape Royds, Mount Erebus is shrouded in cloud in the background.

items.title: Concerns new BHP agreement 'locks in' basin water extraction

children.content: Environmentalists and First Nations groups say a new mining agreement with BHP does not do enough to end water extraction from the Great Artesian Basin. But the SA government says the agreement imposes stricter environmental regulation.

image.altText: An overhead view of a BHP wellfield in outback South Australia.

items.title: Fraud claims and a $400k sculpture: What a secret RSL report found

children.content: The secret report details claims of the misappropriation of funds and fraud, including one of the NSW charity's sub-branches raising money for veterans from the Korean defence force.

image.altText: Returned & Services League Australia logo on the front doors of the RSL Queensland offices in Brisbane.

items.title: Extreme ways young people are trying to secure rentals

children.content: Young Australians are going to extreme lengths to secure rental homes in an over-saturated market, which is leaving "more homeless than ever before".

gallery.altText: A 24-year-old woman and her dog looking at the camera

items.title: David used to ski on the Brisbane River. Now he's too scared of sharks

children.content: The Queensland government is being petitioned to relax fishing limits for bull sharks and consider culls as anglers and waterway users fear numbers are growing.

image.altText: shark being pushed back out into the river

items.title: UK armed forces intercept Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker

children.content: The British armed forces intercept a sanctioned oil tanker in a bid to disrupt the oil revenue ​that helps fund Russia's war in Ukraine.

image.altText: A helicopter hovers over the deck of a large oil tanker out on the ocean

items.title: Japan to roll out nationwide LGBTQIA+ education for the first time

children.content: The country is taking a big step in its efforts to address gay rights but not everyone is convinced it goes far enough.

items.title: Hamilton claims famous Ferrari win, F1 leader suffers late car failure

children.content: Lewis Hamilton has wound back the clock and announced himself as a world championship contender again, claiming his maiden grand prix win as a Ferrari driver.
